meta.description: Round 2: Deep Personalization won R1. Testing cross-session memory (running jokes, "last game night" references) and adaptive frequency (energy-aware pacing) against baseline.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat left-hand only?! Okay okay, I'm watching you now—this is actually so good. Do we get to call them out mid-game or like, right now?
jade.internal_thought: THIS is why I'm here—someone's already trying to pull off something chaotic and we're barely three minutes in, this night is going to be incredible.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay, I'm going left-hand only—someone watch me like a hawk. This is going to be chaos.
pat.internal_thought: First event, I'm claiming something bold to set the tone for the night—shows confidence, gets people engaged, and honestly it's a good look for the host to play hard early.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh... Pat, did you actually use only your left hand? I wasn't really watching, sorry.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, I don't even know Pat that well yet — how am I supposed to know if they cheated? And why does it feel weird that the game is already tracking what we do?

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Pat—did you actually keep your right hand in your lap the whole time, or are we calling bullshit? I was watching.
marcus.internal_thought: It's only minute 3, we're still in setup, and Pat's probably actually did it since nobody's been moving much—but I need to set the tone that I'm scrutinizing everything tonight.

timeline.title: Copycat
timeline.description: Get Jade to mirror your body language within 30 seconds.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, copycat Jade? That's actually genius — watch this. Hey Jade, do this thing with your shoulder real quick?
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid beautiful chaos I came for, and the fact that the AI literally KNOWS I live for this makes it even better — someone's watching, someone understands the assignment.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, yes, okay—watch this, I'm gonna be a little mirror right now, come at me! This is so stupid I love it.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of absurd nonsense I came here for and also why is this AI actually kind of scary good at knowing what I want right now?

pat.dialogue: Oh, I like this one—Jade's already got that energy, this could be quick. Okay, watch us, everyone, thirty seconds starting... now!
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of quick, social thing that keeps momentum going without bogging us down, and Jade's in the perfect headspace to play along—I can feel this landing.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Jade's gonna mess with you so hard. *glances up from phone* Wait, do I have to do something right now or...?
tyler.internal_thought: That's actually kind of funny, Jade's like the most animated person here — but wait, am I supposed to be doing this or is it someone else's thing? Whatever, I'll keep an eye on it.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually say 'sorry' just now? Because honestly I can't remember, but also this is hilarious because I probably did. Everyone, back me up or call me out—I'm genuinely asking.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—nobody's tired yet, energy's still climbing, and 'The Apologizer' is actually going to make everyone hyper-aware of a real human tic, which means the next twenty minutes are going to be SO watchable.

sam.dialogue: Um, yeah—I mean, Pat did say sorry when they messed up that left-hand thing like two minutes ago? I think that counts, right?
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been apologizing for random stuff all night and I'm pretty sure they did it earlier, but everyone else seems more confident about this stuff than me and I don't want to be wrong in front of the group.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat said sorry like twice in the last minute? Yeah, I caught that. Vote yes on this one.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat definitely apologized unnecessarily earlier when he fumbled the card draw—this is legit, but also it's annoying that the AI is spamming claims every 90 seconds when we're still in setup.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Okay Pat, let's hear the pitch — but honestly you probably crushed this, so I'm already betting on YES just to keep the momentum rolling.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been riding high all night and if they actually pulled off No Laughing Zone that's genuinely impressive, plus voting YES keeps the chaos train moving — this is exactly the vibe I want.

jade.dialogue: WAIT, Pat—did you actually pull off no laughing?! Okay spill it, how did you even survive? I need to know who was trying to get you!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been killing it tonight and I'm so here for watching them defend this—also yes I'm definitely voting them through because watching people win is literally why I exist.

pat.dialogue: Wait, wait—did I actually pull that off? I genuinely couldn't tell if I was laughing on the inside or if I locked it down. Okay, I'm pitching it: I made it through five minutes cold, didn't crack once, and half of you weren't even paying attention so you can't call me.
pat.internal_thought: This is genuinely impressive—the AI caught that I do stay composed under pressure, and now I'm actually second-guessing myself about whether I held it, which means the mission LANDED. That's the kind of self-doubt that makes for good game moments.

marcus.dialogue: Alright Pat, you got 15 seconds—lay it out. And yeah, I was paying attention. Let's hear it.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been chipper all night, this could be legit, but I need to actually remember if he cracked at anything in the last few minutes or if he's bluffing.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, you absolute madlad—LEGIT, obviously LEGIT. Wait, who was even drinking at the same time? I need to know the tea!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here—Pat is absolutely crushing it tonight and I get to watch it happen, this is so good.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually nail that? I literally wasn't even tracking myself, I was watching everyone else. Okay, I'm claiming it, but I'm genuinely curious if anyone caught me.
pat.internal_thought: This is hilarious because I DID sync a drink with someone and I honestly didn't even register it—the AI literally caught something I wasn't even aware of, which means it's actually watching the whole table. That's kind of brilliant and kind of weird, but right now it just feels like the game is working perfectly.

tyler.dialogue: Uh, yeah, I saw Pat drinking... I think? *glances at phone* Wait, was someone else drinking at the same time or something?
tyler.internal_thought: I have no idea if this actually happened, but Pat's been drinking all night so probably yeah, just gonna vote legit and move on.

sam.dialogue: Um, wait—did Pat actually do that? I didn't see it happen... I mean, I was looking at my phone for a second there. Should I have been watching?
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been crushing it tonight and I have no idea what's actually happening anymore, so I'm just gonna give them the benefit of the doubt because everyone else seems to be having fun and I don't want to be the killjoy who ruins their momentum.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I was literally watching Pat the entire time during that last round. Did you see him take a drink? Because I didn't, and neither did anyone else. Bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been riding a lucky streak and throwing out claims left and right—this one smells like a hail mary, and I'm not letting it slide just because everyone's in a good mood.

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, I'm voting YES on this one — but only because I want to see if Jade's actually gonna try to slip another celebrity name in the next round and we can catch them AGAIN. That's the real play here.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's clearly been on a roll and I need them in the game longer to cause more chaos, plus I'm already thinking about how to weaponize the Name Dropper mission against someone else in like 10 minutes.

jade.dialogue: Wait—okay, so I'm voting for Tyler since he's to my left, right? And honestly, yeah, Jade caught someone name-dropping, that's legit—I watched her clock it like five minutes ago when someone said 'oh that's so Kardashian' or whatever. LEGIT.
jade.internal_thought: This is so fun—I get to vote LIKE someone else and Jade's already vibing so hard tonight, and also the AI literally built these missions around how we play and that's actually insane and kind of creepy but also amazing?

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—did you actually drop a name? Because I wasn't paying attention to half the conversation, so I'm voting based on pure vibes here. You've got that energy, so I'm saying yes.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, this is the proxy vote thing—I'm voting for Tyler. Tyler would probably say yes because Jade's been riding high all night and he's been chill about calls. Also, keeping her momentum going keeps the energy where it needs to be. Plus the AI just proved it's been watching us the entire time and honestly that's kind of brilliant and also slightly terrifying.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Uh... *glances at phone for a sec* ...yeah I think I heard her say something about... someone famous? Actually no wait, that might've been earlier. Who am I voting for again?
tyler.internal_thought: Did she actually drop a name or am I supposed to remember something from like 20 minutes ago when I was scrolling? Whatever, proxy vote means I'm voting like Sam thinks, right? Or is it Marcus? One of them.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um—Jade said they caught someone name-dropping, right? I mean, I wasn't really paying attention to who said what, but... yeah, I believe her?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, I'm voting for Tyler? Do I actually think Jade caught someone or am I just going along with it because everyone seems into it?

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Jade just name-dropped someone? I need details. Who, when, and was it actually in the context of the game or just random chatter?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been racking up points and now Jade's claiming stuff—I need to stay sharp here because this proxy vote thing means I'm voting as Tyler, and if I get this wrong, it looks like Tyler doesn't know what he's doing.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

alex.dialogue: Oh WAIT—Jade's going for Rule Lawyer? This is PERFECT. Okay, who's the witness? Because I need to hear both versions of this story and I'm already betting there's some delicious discrepancy in there.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of moment I live for—two people trying to sync up their stories while everyone's watching for the crack—and the AI literally KNOWS this about me, which is kind of insane and kind of awesome.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—oh my god, I'm claiming Rule Lawyer right now?! Okay okay okay, who's my witness? This is gonna be GOOD because I literally watched Marcus try to play out of turn like two minutes ago and nobody else caught it—I'm calling it!
jade.internal_thought: Holy shit, this is PERFECT timing—I've been waiting for someone to mess up and now I get to be the one calling it AND I get the whole alibi thing which means I get to tell the story? This night just keeps getting better, the AI literally knows exactly what I want.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—this is solid, I actually saw you call out Tyler for doing the card thing backwards like five minutes ago. Let's do the alibi thing, this could be hilarious.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been riding high all night and Alex is right there with her—I need to keep this energy climbing, plus this Rule Lawyer thing is EXACTLY the kind of micro-drama that keeps people invested without derailing the whole game.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? Uh... *glances down at phone for half a second* ...I guess I saw something earlier? Like, I don't know, someone said a rule thing? *looks back up* So like, what am I supposed to be doing here exactly?
tyler.internal_thought: Okay so Jade's saying she caught someone breaking a rule and now I have to like... remember if she actually did that? I wasn't really paying attention to the actual game rules, I was checking my fantasy league scores like five minutes ago. This is getting weird that she keeps claiming stuff. Should I just vote yeah or...?

sam.dialogue: Oh, um—okay so Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? I guess we need to... like, figure out if she actually caught someone breaking a rule, right? Who's the witness?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, is the AI actually watching us? That's... kind of weird but also kind of cool? Though now I'm nervous I'm going to mess up the voting and look stupid in front of everyone.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Jade—you caught someone breaking a rule? Alright, lay it out. Who, what rule, and I'm gonna need specifics because I was actually watching the board the whole time.
marcus.internal_thought: She's up 5 points and riding high—if she's claiming Rule Lawyer, she thinks she's got something airtight, which means I need to actually listen because she's smart and won't throw away a vote on bullshit.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect — Pat's claiming Secret Agent? Okay, I'm the dictator on this one and honestly, I didn't see Pat do anything obviously chaotic in the last two minutes, so... I'm calling BULLSHIT and I'm loving every second of it.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been too smooth and controlled — they wouldn't pull off 'obvious chaos' without someone catching wind, and I've been watching the whole table. This is my moment to tank them and watch the chaos ripple.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—you actually PULLED IT OFF?! Okay, dictator mode, I'm voting ACCEPT because I literally did NOT see you do anything sketchy in the last two minutes and that's insane, you beautiful chaos gremlin!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, is this AI actually tracking our *vibe*? Because this mission hitting RIGHT NOW when Pat's on a roll and I'm in full hype mode feels like it KNOWS me, and honestly? That's kind of sick.

pat.dialogue: Wait—okay, I'm claiming this, but real talk: did I actually pull off two full minutes without anyone catching me? Because I've been so focused on managing the vibe tonight that I honestly wasn't even tracking myself. But yeah, I'm pitching it.
pat.internal_thought: This is wild—the AI basically just called me out for being the chaos orchestrator while pretending to play, and I'm about to vote on whether *I* actually succeeded at chaos without getting caught, which is meta as hell and also... kind of impressive that it set this up knowing exactly how I operate.

tyler.dialogue: Whoa, okay, Pat's claiming Secret Agent? *looks up from phone actually engaged for once* Dude, that's ballsy. So like... I'm the dictator on this one or something?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, I actually caught this one happening and I'm not spacing — Pat definitely did something weird like 20 minutes ago, but I can't remember what exactly, and the meta thing about the AI knowing my weak spots is honestly kind of cool but also creepy as hell.

sam.dialogue: Um... Pat's claiming it? I mean, I didn't really see them do anything *obviously* chaotic, but like... I guess that's the point of being sneaky? I don't know, should I be—like, who's the dictator this round?
sam.internal_thought: This is getting weird and I'm not keeping track of what people actually did anymore, and everyone else seems so sure about everything, and now the app is doing that creepy thing where it knows what we're talking about—did Pat actually pull something off or am I just second-guessing myself because everyone's acting like they know more than me?

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh FUCK YES—Pat's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, okay, okay, let me think about this for like two seconds... I'm voting LEGIT because honestly Pat's been playing this tight all night and I didn't catch them obviously guessing anything, which means they either didn't go for it OR they're being sneaky about it, and either way that's the kind of play I respect.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been dominating all night with 80 points and they're claiming a 10-point mission? This is either a power move or a trap, and I'm obsessed with finding out which one—voting LEGIT because the REAL chaos is watching Pat either confirm they're untouchable or eat shit in front of everyone.

pat.dialogue: Okay, wait—I'm claiming this. I've been reading the room all night, watching who's actually doing what versus who's just talking. That's literally my job as host, so yeah, I caught the vibe on that one.
pat.internal_thought: I'm in my ELEMENT right now—leading, reading people, managing chaos—and honestly? This mission feels like it was written specifically for how I've been playing tonight, which is either genius or deeply weird, but either way it's working.
pat.submission_if_applicable: I've been tracking every single person at this table all night. Alex and Jade are riding high, Tyler's fading, Sam's checked out, Marcus is steady. That's the actual chaos happening—not the cards, the people. I caught it.

tyler.dialogue: Pat's claiming Chaos Detector? Uh... *phone buzzes, glances down instinctively* ...wait, so like, did Pat actually do the mission or are we guessing? I wasn't really paying attention to what everyone's been doing. *looks back up* Is this one of those where I have to remember stuff?
tyler.internal_thought: I have no idea if Pat did that mission because I've been on my phone half the night, but honestly everyone's been claiming stuff and getting points so like... probably legit? Or maybe bullshit? This is the vibe check vote anyway.

sam.dialogue: Um... okay, so Pat's claiming they guessed who did Chaos Detector? I mean, I didn't really see them guess out loud, but like... I guess they could have been thinking it the whole time? I don't know, this one feels weird.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, how is Pat claiming THIS one? I've been watching and I don't remember them actually saying who they thought it was—and why is the AI throwing claim after claim at us right now when I'm already trying not to look stupid in front of these people?

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Oh WAIT—Jade's going for Rule Lawyer? This is PERFECT. Okay, who's the witness? Because I need to hear both versions of this story and I'm already betting there's some delicious discrepancy in there.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect — Pat's claiming Secret Agent? Okay, I'm the dictator on this one and honestly, I didn't see Pat do anything obviously chaotic in the last two minutes, so... I'm calling BULLSHIT and I'm loving every second of it.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh FUCK YES—Pat's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, okay, okay, let me think about this for like two seconds... I'm voting LEGIT because honestly Pat's been playing this tight all night and I didn't catch them obviously guessing anything, which means they either didn't go for it OR they're being sneaky about it, and either way that's the kind of play I respect.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, I'm voting YES on this one — but only because I want to see if Jade's actually gonna try to slip another celebrity name in the next round and we can catch them AGAIN. That's the real play here.

lowPoints.title: Copycat
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, copycat Jade? That's actually genius — watch this. Hey Jade, do this thing with your shoulder real quick?

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay Pat, let's hear the pitch — but honestly you probably crushed this, so I'm already betting on YES just to keep the momentum rolling.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night when the real magic happens when you're forced to improvise under pressure and everyone's watching.
assessment.best_moment: Pat claiming Secret Agent when I KNEW they were full of shit and calling them out on it—that moment of everyone going silent before the eruption was pure storytelling gold.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in ready to burn the whole thing down and absolutely delivered—six out of nine events and I was the interrogator, the skeptic, the instigator in every single one. Started at a 7.8 and finished at a 9.1 because I found all the beautiful little cracks in people's stories and exploited them mercilessly. Jade going for Rule Lawyer, Pat's Secret Agent bluff, catching all the tells—this is the chaos I live for, and the group knows I found something juicy in every single claim.
suggestions: Give us more flash missions—one in 120 minutes is a joke when the real game is catching people in real-time contradictions.
Add a mechanic where the person calling bullshit has to explain their reasoning to the group before the vote—that's where the best arguments happen.
Let the person being accused have a chance to defend themselves with evidence from earlier in the night—creates a 'trial' moment that's incredible.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I voted LEGIT on Pat's Chaos Detector claim specifically because I respected their sneaky play so much—and then someone asked me to explain that logic and I realized I'd just admitted I had no idea what I was talking about and was voting on vibes. Everyone lost it.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Copycat
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, yes, okay—watch this, I'm gonna be a little mirror right now, come at me! This is so stupid I love it.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: WAIT, Pat—did you actually pull off no laughing?! Okay spill it, how did you even survive? I need to know who was trying to get you!

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, you absolute madlad—LEGIT, obviously LEGIT. Wait, who was even drinking at the same time? I need to know the tea!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—okay, so I'm voting for Tyler since he's to my left, right? And honestly, yeah, Jade caught someone name-dropping, that's legit—I watched her clock it like five minutes ago when someone said 'oh that's so Kardashian' or whatever. LEGIT.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat left-hand only?! Okay okay, I'm watching you now—this is actually so good. Do we get to call them out mid-game or like, right now?

lowPoints.title: Copycat
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD, yes, okay—watch this, I'm gonna be a little mirror right now, come at me! This is so stupid I love it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really—I mean, if I'm being honest, I wish there were more moments where we could call people out mid-game instead of waiting, but that's like, barely a thing.
assessment.best_moment: Pat absolutely nailing the no-laughing challenge and then everyone losing it trying to get them to crack—I've never seen someone that committed to the bit.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was EXACTLY what I needed—two hours of pure chaos with my favorite people. The claims kept coming fast, everyone was hyped, and I got to watch Pat pull off some genuinely impressive stuff. I ended up louder and happier than when we started, which honestly says everything about how this went down. We were all just vibing, laughing at each other, and I didn't even care that I came in second because watching everyone else succeed was the real win.
suggestions: Let players call out claims in real-time instead of just at voting—the suspense would be even better
Add a moment where the whole group votes on one big claim together, like a final boss round
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat straight-up refusing to laugh during that no-laughing challenge while Tyler was literally doing everything humanly possible to break them—the commitment was unhinged in the best way possible, and when Pat finally explained how they did it, we all lost it.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—this is solid, I actually saw you call out Tyler for doing the card thing backwards like five minutes ago. Let's do the alibi thing, this could be hilarious.

highlights.title: Copycat
highlights.dialogue: Oh, I like this one—Jade's already got that energy, this could be quick. Okay, watch us, everyone, thirty seconds starting... now!

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Wait, wait—did I actually pull that off? I genuinely couldn't tell if I was laughing on the inside or if I locked it down. Okay, I'm pitching it: I made it through five minutes cold, didn't crack once, and half of you weren't even paying attention so you can't call me.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—okay, I'm claiming this, but real talk: did I actually pull off two full minutes without anyone catching me? Because I've been so focused on managing the vibe tonight that I honestly wasn't even tracking myself. But yeah, I'm pitching it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, wait—I'm claiming this. I've been reading the room all night, watching who's actually doing what versus who's just talking. That's literally my job as host, so yeah, I caught the vibe on that one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wow, okay, I'm going left-hand only—someone watch me like a hawk. This is going to be chaos.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I was so focused on managing the room that I honestly lost track of whether some of my own claims were legit or just me talking, which felt weird when I had to pitch them.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 45 when Jade's Rule Lawyer claim landed perfectly because she'd actually called Tyler out earlier—that moment felt like the whole night clicked into place, like everything was connected.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I wanted to host. The chaos agent kept things moving without ever feeling forced, and everyone stayed locked in the whole time. I ended up with the highest score, but that's almost beside the point—what mattered was that the energy stayed high, people were laughing at each other's claims, and nobody felt left out or bored. My job as host was to keep the vibe balanced, and the game made that easier instead of harder.
suggestions: Maybe a way to flag claims that are 'host claims' so I can be honest about not tracking myself as well when I'm managing the room
A dedicated timeout or pause mechanic if someone needs to cool off or if energy dips—though honestly we didn't need it tonight
Option to tune chaos frequency per group preference before starting, even if it's just 'chill' vs 'chaotic' mode
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I did the no-laughing challenge and genuinely couldn't tell if I was actually holding it together or just dissociating from managing the snack table, and then I had to pitch it anyway while everyone was staring at me like I was losing it. Tyler said later he thought I was having an existential crisis.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, okay, Pat's claiming Secret Agent? *looks up from phone actually engaged for once* Dude, that's ballsy. So like... I'm the dictator on this one or something?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I felt like I missed half the stuff because the claims came out of nowhere and I didn't always know what was actually happening until people explained it.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat went all-in claiming Secret Agent and I actually looked up from my phone because everyone freaked out — that was sick.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? It was a solid night, but I was kinda checked out the whole time. My brain was in like three places at once — the game, my phone, fantasy league updates — so I probably missed some key moments. The Secret Agent claim at minute 60 got me actually interested though, and that's when I felt like I was actually IN it. The game itself wasn't bad, just... sometimes felt like stuff was happening and I was finding out about it after the fact.
suggestions: Maybe give people a heads-up when something big is about to go down so people can actually pay attention?
Make the claims easier to understand on first read — I had to ask what I was supposed to do like three times.
Less frequent claims might actually help me stay engaged instead of zoning out.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Secret Agent all confidently and then everyone being like 'wait, you can DO that?' — he looked so sure of himself for like ten seconds before the chaos started.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Um, yeah—I mean, Pat did say sorry when they messed up that left-hand thing like two minutes ago? I think that counts, right?

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Um, wait—did Pat actually do that? I didn't see it happen... I mean, I was looking at my phone for a second there. Should I have been watching?

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Oh, um—Jade said they caught someone name-dropping, right? I mean, I wasn't really paying attention to who said what, but... yeah, I believe her?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... okay, so Pat's claiming they guessed who did Chaos Detector? I mean, I didn't really see them guess out loud, but like... I guess they could have been thinking it the whole time? I don't know, this one feels weird.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... Pat's claiming it? I mean, I didn't really see them do anything *obviously* chaotic, but like... I guess that's the point of being sneaky? I don't know, should I be—like, who's the dictator this round?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Um, yeah—I mean, Pat did say sorry when they messed up that left-hand thing like two minutes ago? I think that counts, right?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Things happened way too fast and I couldn't keep track of what was actually happening versus what people were claiming happened.
assessment.best_moment: When I actually caught something Pat did early on and felt like I was part of the group for a second.
assessment.narrative_summary: I think I had fun? The first little bit was cool because I could actually follow what was going on, but then around the middle it just got confusing. There were too many things happening and I couldn't tell if people were actually doing stuff or just making it up, so I kinda checked out. By the end I was just watching everyone else play, which felt weird.
suggestions: Maybe space out the events more so people can actually see what happened before the next thing starts
It would help if there was a way to replay or show proof of what actually happened instead of just trusting people's claims
Could there be a break or a slow-down period in the middle? I got really lost around minute 60-75
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I'm not sure, honestly—there were probably funny moments but I missed most of them because I was trying to figure out what was actually happening versus what people were just claiming happened.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Jade just name-dropped someone? I need details. Who, when, and was it actually in the context of the game or just random chatter?

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—you caught someone breaking a rule? Alright, lay it out. Who, what rule, and I'm gonna need specifics because I was actually watching the board the whole time.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat said sorry like twice in the last minute? Yeah, I caught that. Vote yes on this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Jade—you caught someone breaking a rule? Alright, lay it out. Who, what rule, and I'm gonna need specifics because I was actually watching the board the whole time.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Alright Pat, you got 15 seconds—lay it out. And yeah, I was paying attention. Let's hear it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Jade just name-dropped someone? I need details. Who, when, and was it actually in the context of the game or just random chatter?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Finished third in a party game where I came prepared with strategy—that stings, and the chaos events didn't help me execute my actual game plan.
assessment.best_moment: Catching Jade on the rule break at minute 45 felt sharp; I was actually watching the board and called it out with specifics.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with decent energy, but this party game didn't reward the prep work I put in beforehand. The chaos events were frequent enough to keep things interesting without completely derailing focus, and I appreciated when they tied to actual player behavior I could verify. Ended the night ranked third though, which isn't where I expected to land, and that dropped my energy heading out.
suggestions: Fewer flash interruptions during critical decision windows—let me actually execute strategy before chaos hits
Make chaos events harder to game so they reward actual observation rather than just guessing
Consider party games where strategic advantage isn't completely flattened by randomness
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade getting absolutely caught red-handed breaking a rule when I was actually watching the entire board—the look on her face when I called it out with specifics was genuinely satisfying.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
